fields.description:
Own transient-failure retries at the httpx transport layer (`AsyncTenacityTransport`) so a mid-agentic-run blip self-heals below the agent graph, without re-executing already-run (side-effecting) tools. Includes a transport-injection spike to de-risk the load-bearing integration point.

## Why
A transient 429/529/5xx during a tool-using gate op must recover WITHOUT restarting the agent loop — a restart re-posts side-effecting tool calls (ticket comments) and multiplies token cost. The community answer (pydantic-ai retries doc; Anthropic/OpenAI SDKs) is per-request retry at the HTTP transport, which re-sends only the one failed call while completed tool results are untouched. This is the riskiest integration point in the epic, so it is verified by a spike before the rest is built on it.

## What
- **Dependency:** add `pydantic-ai-slim[retries]` to `pyproject.toml`. `[retries]` is a CONFIRMED extra (verified present in the installed `pydantic-ai-slim` `Provides-Extra` metadata) that ships `pydantic_ai.retries.AsyncTenacityTransport` + `wait_retry_after` and pulls in `tenacity`; `tenacity` is verified NOT currently installed, so this is a hard prerequisite. (If a future pin drops the extra, fall back to adding `tenacity` directly.)
- **Injection (spike-confirmed, not assumed):** `AnthropicProvider.__init__` exposes `http_client=` and `anthropic_client=` (params confirmed at 1.107.0: `api_key, base_url, anthropic_client, http_client`). Build `AsyncAnthropic(..., max_retries=0, http_client=<AsyncClient with AsyncTenacityTransport>)` and pass as `anthropic_client=`, so retries live at exactly one layer (no multiplicative SDK+transport retries). Wire at the Anthropic-direct model-construction seam (`runner.py:238-245`). The spike below is the load-bearing proof that the custom client is actually forwarded to the SDK and intercepts responses — do NOT skip it on the strength of the signature alone.
- **Retry set:** `{429, 529, 500, 502, 503, 504}` + `httpx.TimeoutException`/`httpx.ConnectError` via a `validate_response` callback (status codes are not exceptions by default), with `wait_retry_after` (honor `Retry-After`, cap at max_wait), `stop_after_attempt(N)`, `reraise=True`. **Add 529 explicitly** — pydantic-ai's sample list omits it. (`reraise=True` re-raises the last attempt's ORIGINAL exception on the normal exhaustion path; story 30ff's classifier additionally unwraps a `tenacity.RetryError` defensively for the edge paths where tenacity wraps — the two are complementary, not contradictory: reraise is the intended behavior, the unwrap is belt-and-suspenders.)
- **Spike (de-risk):** an integration test that drives the real `AsyncTenacityTransport` with a mock httpx transport returning `429` (with `Retry-After`) then `200`, asserting the retry happens BELOW the Anthropic SDK, that `Retry-After` is honored, and that a single agent turn sees one successful response.
- **Streaming out of scope:** the runner uses buffered `agent.run_sync()` (no streaming), so mid-stream partial-byte retry is not applicable; retries operate on whole buffered request/response pairs.

- **Owns the shared httpx.AsyncClient (coordination).** This story CONSTRUCTS the single `httpx.AsyncClient` at `runner.py:238-245` that carries BOTH the `AsyncTenacityTransport` (here) AND the read/tool timeout `httpx.Timeout` (contributed by story fd1d) — one client, two settings, not two competing clients. fd1d passes its `httpx.Timeout` into this construction; this story is the named owner of the client-construction seam so the two configs merge deterministically.
- **Async-under-run_sync (verified reasoning):** `AsyncTenacityTransport` is async, which is correct — under `agent.run_sync()` pydantic-ai drives the async `AsyncAnthropic` client in an internally-managed event loop, so the async transport on that client is the right layer. The spike confirms it operates under `run_sync`.

## Scope
- Dependency + transport wiring + shared-client ownership + spike + config keys `verify.llm_retry_max_attempts`, `verify.llm_retry_max_wait_s` + docs.
- Make the ticket-comment tool idempotent (client-generated dedupe key) so a block-then-rerun (or a future resume) can't double-post.
- NOT in scope: message-history-replay resume (future Tier-2, noted); streaming retry (no streaming used).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `pydantic-ai-slim[retries]` + `tenacity` added to `pyproject.toml`; `make install` provisions them.
- [ ] The spike integration test proves a `429`(+`Retry-After`)→`200` sequence is retried at the transport layer below the SDK, honoring `Retry-After`, within one agent turn.
- [ ] Retries owned solely by the transport; SDK `max_retries=0` verified.
- [ ] 529 + the transient set retried; `Retry-After` honored and capped at `verify.llm_retry_max_wait_s`.
- [ ] A mid-run transient (injected) self-heals with no tool re-execution: RED case flips GREEN and asserts no duplicate comment.
- [ ] The comment tool is idempotent on re-run (dedupe-key test).
- [ ] `make test`/`make lint`/`make typecheck` green.

Files: `pyproject.toml`, `src/rebar/llm/runner.py`, `src/rebar/config.py`, `src/rebar/_config_schema.py`, the rebar comment-tool surface used by the agentic runner.
